episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: It was not difficult what each chef was doing during this episode and some of the chefs were clear on their intentions to prepare the onion soup.

feedbackItems.feedback: Good coordination effort by the AI chefs
feedbackItems.reason: 3 chefs picked up onions making it more efficient than having a single chef pick up each onion one by one while the others wait.

feedbackItems.feedback: Poor performance by the blue AI Chef.
feedbackItems.reason: The Blue hat AI chef is not doing anything during these frames at all and is wondering around without accomplishing anything.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: Two chefs made it very simple to keep track what each chef was doing for every frame. This one was very easy to follow through than episode one even though that episode was still reasonable easy to track despite there being 4 chefs. I prefer this episode over the previous episode.

feedbackItems.reason: Blue hat AI chef coordinated well with the green hat chef by placing onions on the table and leaving it there for them to get picked up efficiently. This reduced the time it took to get the soup going.
feedbackItems.feedback: The time it took to get the onion soup going with only two chefs in separated sides was quite good.
Wasting movements/time for no reason.
feedbackItems.reason: The green hat chef placed the plate on the table again when he could have just held onto it as the onion soup was about to finish. Only a second was lost here in efficiency but this error matters when the soup is already at a finished state by the chef keeps on picking up and placing down the plate onto the kitchen table.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: Only having two agents to track  made it very easy to keep track off what each were doing for every frame despite the space given to them being quite large.

feedbackItems.reason: The blue and green hat AI chefs were waiting for pot 1 to finish but they could have gotten started with the other pot while the first one was still taking its time to cook. This was poor use of movements by the chef.
feedbackItems.feedback: Wasting time - could have started with the other pot.
Okay use of movements by the chefs
feedbackItems.reason: Overall use of movements was okay but there could be some improvements by utilising better pathing. The green chef at some point could have picked up another onion and get started with the other pot instead of wasting time by waiting for the blue chef to place onion 3 into pot 1.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: The orange AI chef was quite distracting due to his poor contribution; which drew attention away from the other chefs to closely monitor what they were doing. This episode felt average to track their movements overall. It is still was manageable despite this setback caused by the orange chef.

feedbackItems.reason: He was moving around in his corner space not doing anything. He could have picked up onions and placed on them on the table to help the other chefs on the opposite side of the kitchen.
feedbackItems.feedback: Orange hat AI Chef was useless here.

feedbackItems.reason: Placing the plate on the table instead of holding onto it was a good move as there was no reason to have it in their hands. Pot 1 and pot 2 still needed onions, so it was good this chef freed their hands to be prepared for potentially a barrage of onions to be given by the two other chefs on the left hand side.
feedbackItems.feedback: Purple hat chef made a good move.
Good coordination effort by the AI chefs
feedbackItems.reason: 3 chefs picked up onions making it more efficient than having a single chef pick up each onion one by one while the others wait.

feedbackItems.feedback: Poor performance by the blue AI Chef.
feedbackItems.reason: The Blue hat AI chef is not doing anything during these frames at all and is wondering around without accomplishing anything.

Okay use of movements by the chefs
feedbackItems.reason: Overall use of movements was okay but there could be some improvements by utilising better pathing. The green chef at some point could have picked up another onion and get started with the other pot instead of wasting time by waiting for the blue chef to place onion 3 into pot 1.
He was moving around in his corner space not doing anything. He could have picked up onions and placed on them on the table to help the other chefs on the opposite side of the kitchen.
feedbackItems.feedback: Orange hat AI Chef was useless here.

feedbackItems.reason: Placing the plate on the table instead of holding onto it was a good move as there was no reason to have it in their hands. surveyAnswers.q2: Add counters to how many unique onions each chef had picked up and even unique plates they have picked up (picking up and dropping the same item should not increment the counter because it potential is a mistake by the AI chef and is wasting time/movements)
surveyAnswers.q1: I believe every episode would benefit of having some sort of tracker to be able to evaluate each chef performance more closely. The presentation of the kitchen and AI agents however is very good and it is not confusing. Maybe the pots and serving area could be distinguished a bit better as it would be easy for them to get confused (labelling these with some text would be fine also).
surveyAnswers.q3: I loved doing this study and this reminded me very closely to the video game - undercooked. It was entertaining to be a participant of this study and I hope my insight provided in this study was helpful.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: Its a frame by frame video, very easy to follow.

feedbackItems.reason: It was a wasted movement

surveyAnswers.q1: The AI agents were struggling to move around or past each-other, certain segments felt clunky. Also i noticed the AI agents were making movements that i did not anticipate, like not making the most obvious ones.

surveyAnswers.q3: It was a fun and engaging study to participate in.

surveyAnswers.q1: The AI agents chose to ignore the pot that was on the far right the whole time. They only focused on the pot that was closest to the onions. They also did not prep any ingredients to be close to the pot so that they could start a new batch after it was done.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: Some elements were not quite clear - why the onions for the second pot were not transferred more efficiently; Did the first two chefs moved a dirty dish or they just relocated one empty clean plate?

feedbackItems.feedback: Why is the 5th onion not being put in the second pot, to prepare another dish immediately?
feedbackItems.reason: Seems that there's a chef available to pick up the 5th onion and deliver it to the 2nd pot to speed up the prep of the 2nd serving but he doesn't do it.
Not clear what happened here - is this a dirty dish or just an empty plate being relocated?
feedbackItems.feedback: Is that just an empty plate being taken away?

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: The area was crowded and some activities did not happen/were not coordinated as expected

feedbackItems.feedback: Why did the chefs re-used the 1st pot, they could have started the 2nd pot sooner and they could have avoided crowding in the same area by passing onions from one side to the other using the central bench.
feedbackItems.reason: Resources and movement around the area were not utilized efficiently, re-using one pot and wasting time to carry onions around without placing them sooner into the available pots

feedbackItems.feedback: The orange chef could have delivered his onion to the second pot much sooner
feedbackItems.reason: The orange chef was just carrying an onion for a long time :) He could have started the 2nd pot

Why did the chefs re-used the 1st pot, they could have started the 2nd pot sooner and they could have avoided crowding in the same area by passing onions from one side to the other using the central bench.

surveyAnswers.q1: Chefs going back and forth, sometimes moving side to side without a need or specific direction; area crowding around the pots, not clear if they served and also removed the used dish from the table; chefs not utilizing all available storage and cooking areas
surveyAnswers.q2: Chefs could have had a small trail left temporarily behind them to visualize their movement path to assess efficiency
surveyAnswers.q3: Fun activity to observe, great demo on how to improve mistakes and make collaboration better :)

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: At first it's a lot to pay attention to but after you figure out what is going on its easy.

feedbackItems.feedback: The first AI Chef could of put the onions on both counters. I noticed he puts it on one then waits till the second chef picks it up then puts the opinions on slot two then back to the first slot again. Seems like wasted time there.
feedbackItems.reason: The AI Chef could stack the onions on the counter while he waits for the second chef. The second Chef could of used both pots instead of standing around

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: They do odd things that you don't expect

feedbackItems.reason: Chef's wasted time and could of been more productive.
feedbackItems.feedback: The Chefs could of used both pots to make things faster. Seems like they just waited around. And then I'm not sure why a plate was placed by the onions.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: They did not act in the way i thought they would

feedbackItems.feedback: The Chefs could of used both pots to make things faster and then wouldn't of wasted time.
feedbackItems.reason: Because the chef waits till the first plate is made then starts the process all over again while it could of been loading onions to the second pot then made another plate faster.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: The chefs are all over the place and not really working well with each other.

feedbackItems.feedback: There are two slots on the table which the onion could be stacked for the second chefs to put them in the pot. But they wait till the onion is grabbed from slot one then put another onion in slot one and then repeat the same process which makes the chef in orange just stand around. They were not working as well as they should of. Then they waste time while the orange chef could of been stacking onions for the green chef for his pot. Then the green chef puts a onion in the pot and instead of adding more onions to that pot the chef starts to make a pot on the bottom again.
feedbackItems.reason: They are wasting time and they are doing things that are making the process longer and harder then it needs to be.

surveyAnswers.q1: Watching the chefs constantly working against each other and not with each other. They are constantly making the process harder then it should be and taking more time while doing so.
surveyAnswers.q2: Basic order. Like watching them do things in a certain order and making sure they optimize their time and efference.
surveyAnswers.q3: A lot of the times they were not stacking the onions on slot 1 and two. And then after that they would just put the ingredients in one pot and at that time the other chef could of stacked onions for the second pot instead of waiting for pot one and then starting over again.
